name: Naphthalene
plain_summary: Two fused benzene rings (mothball smell). Absorbs deeper into the UV than benzene.

spectra.plain_caption: Strong UV bands ~221–286 nm. Still mostly colorless as a solid film.

source.citation: Strong UV bands ~221–286 nm. Still mostly colorless as a solid film.
source.license: CC-BY-4.0 packaging; verify experimental values against primary literature for research use.

spectra.plain_caption: IR fingerprint: out-of-plane ~750; C=C ring ~1600. Peaks mark how bonds stretch and bend.

source.citation: Educational multi-Gaussian IR envelope from typical functional-group wavenumbers (not a raw FTIR file).

spectra.plain_caption: Raman shifts: ring ~1600; ring breathing ~1000. Raman highlights polarizable bonds (teaching envelope).

source.citation: Educational multi-Gaussian Raman envelope from typical shifts (not a raw spectrometer file).

spectra.plain_caption: AA′BB′ aromatic system — two multiplets near 7.5 and 7.8 ppm.

source.citation: Teaching ¹H/¹³C chemical shifts after standard organic spectroscopy tables (Pretsch / Pavia-style compilations). Multiplet envelopes are simulated from δ and J — not raw FIDs or instrument SI.

spectra.plain_caption: Three ¹³C environments for unsubstituted naphthalene.

source.citation: Teaching ¹H/¹³C chemical shifts after standard organic spectroscopy tables (Pretsch / Pavia-style compilations). Multiplet envelopes are simulated from δ and J — not raw FIDs or instrument SI.

spectra.plain_caption: Naphthalene EI: very strong M+• 128 (stable aromatic).

source.citation: Teaching MS peak lists after common organic mass spectrometry tables (McLafferty-style / textbook EI patterns). Relative intensities and even some fragment assignments vary across papers and instruments — verify primary literature for research. Not vendor raw files.
source.note: Tier A teaching MS — consolidated schematic, not multi-paper consensus
